title: Orthodon grosseserratus
taxonomy: Orthodon grosseserratus is a species of fish in the family Odontodidae. It is a type of extinct fish that belongs to the genus Orthodon. The classification of Orthodon grosseserratus is as follows: Kingdom: Animalia, Phylum: Chordata, Class: Actinopterygii, Order: Phyllolepidiformes, Family: Odontodidae, Genus: Orthodon, Species: O. grosseserratus. This ancient fish is known for its distinctive dental features and is often found in fossil records from the Carboniferous period. Its classification reflects its evolutionary position within the ancient fish groups.

intro: Orthodon grosseserratus, also known as Toothache Plant, is a perennial herb native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, offering relief from toothaches and other oral health issues, as well as potential benefits for reducing fever and alleviating pain. The bioactive constituents of Orthodon grosseserratus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made from the Toothache Plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its health benefits.
